name: Brahmananda Sarasvati
dates_notes: Disciple-line of Madhusūdana; dated by manuscript chronology to the 17th–18th c.
core_thesis: Gauḍa-Brahmānanda Sarasvatī (c. 1650–1750) writes within Madhusūdana's disciple-line, defending the *Advaita-Siddhi* against subsequent Mādhva attack. He composes the *Laghu-Candrikā* (also called the *Gauḍa-Brahmānandī*) as a sustained reply to the second-stage Mādhva attack on Advaita launched by the Vyāsatīrtha school — most notably the *Nyāyāmṛta-Tarangiṇī* sub-commentary tradition that targets the *Advaita-Siddhi* point by point. He inherits the Vivaraṇa positions in their Madhusūdana-refined form: Brahman as locus of *bhāva-rūpā-anirvacanīya-avidyā* (*Laghu-Candrikā* on *Advaita-Siddhi* prathama-paricheda, *avidyā-svarūpa-vicāra*); *anirvacanīya-mithyātva* (*Laghu-Candrikā* on *Advaita-Siddhi* prathama-paricheda, *pañca-mithyātva-lakṣaṇa-vicāra*); *jīva* as *cidābhāsa* (*Laghu-Candrikā* on *Advaita-Siddhi* tṛtīya-paricheda, *jīva-svarūpa-vicāra*). He develops them with the technical resources of mature Navya-Nyāya, matching the Mādhva opponents' terminological precision. His reply makes the *Advaita-Siddhi* / *Nyāyāmṛta* exchange tri-generational — Madhusūdana, then Vyāsatīrtha's school, then Brahmānanda — and keeps the polemic live; both sides are preserved and read together to this day. He is to be sharply distinguished from the modern Brahmānanda Sarasvatī (1871–1953), Śaṅkarācārya of Jyotirmath.

engaged_works.title: Laghu-Chandrika on Advaita-Siddhi
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored sub-commentary on the Advaita-Siddhi.
engaged_works.summary: Compact ('*laghu*') sub-commentary on Madhusūdana's *Advaita-Siddhi*, written explicitly to defend Madhusūdana against the second-stage Mādhva critique launched in the *Tarangiṇī* tradition descended from Vyāsatīrtha. Brahmānanda works the *Advaita-Siddhi*'s arguments through the technical apparatus of mature Navya-Nyāya, matching the Mādhva opponents' precision. He supplies point-by-point replies on each contested sub-thesis: the *anirvacanīya* status of avidyā and of *mithyā* (*Laghu-Candrikā* on *Advaita-Siddhi* prathama-paricheda, *anirvacanīyatā-vicāra*); the *bhāva-rūpā* analysis of ignorance (*Laghu-Candrikā* on *Advaita-Siddhi* prathama-paricheda, *avidyā-svarūpa*); the *dṛśyatva-* and *jaḍatva-anumānas* (*Laghu-Candrikā* on *Advaita-Siddhi* dvitīya-paricheda, *mithyātva-anumāna-vicāra*); the analysis of *bheda* (*Laghu-Candrikā* on *Advaita-Siddhi* dvitīya-paricheda, *bheda-khaṇḍana-vicāra*); the locus of avidyā (*Laghu-Candrikā* on *Advaita-Siddhi* tṛtīya-paricheda, *āśraya-vicāra*). The *Laghu-Candrikā* extends the *Advaita-Siddhi* / *Nyāyāmṛta* polemic into a third generation. Together with Madhusūdana's reply to Vyāsatīrtha and the later Mādhva responses, it constitutes one of the most sustained and technically demanding multi-generational exchanges in Indian philosophy, with both sides preserved and read together to this day.

engaged_works.title: Gauda-Brahmanandi on Advaita-Siddhi
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored extended sub-commentary on the Advaita-Siddhi.
engaged_works.summary: The extended sub-commentary on Madhusūdana's *Advaita-Siddhi*, larger and more discursive than the *Laghu-Candrikā* (with which the title is sometimes used interchangeably in the manuscript tradition). Brahmānanda traces each of the *Advaita-Siddhi*'s arguments at length (*Gauḍa-Brahmānandī* on all four *parichedas*: prathama-, dvitīya-, tṛtīya-, caturtha-paricheda), integrates the *Laghu-Candrikā*'s replies to the *Tarangiṇī* tradition, and gives the most thorough single Vivaraṇa-line treatment of the *Advaita-Siddhi*'s technical positions. The work is the standard reference for advanced study of the *Advaita-Siddhi*. Any modern reading of the *Advaita-Siddhi* / *Nyāyāmṛta* exchange uses Brahmānanda's apparatus to navigate Madhusūdana's compressed dialectic, and most published editions carry the *Gauḍa-Brahmānandī* alongside the root text.
